title: Classic nodular basal cell carcinoma on forearm in a 62-year-old female

patient.chief_complaint: Slowly growing skin lesion on the left forearm

1.setting: Primary care visit evaluating suspicious skin lesion on the forearm.
1.capabilities: History, physical examination, and close visual inspection are available.

2.capabilities: Can perform dermoscopic evaluation and minor procedures such as shave biopsy.

3.setting: Follow-up after biopsy with definitive pathology available.
3.capabilities: Pathology review complete and treatment planning for excision.

demographics.valid_request_if: Asked for demographics or baseline characteristics.
demographics.content:
Age 62
Sex Female
Skin phototype II
No immunosuppression
No anticoagulation
No relevant comorbidities
Lives independently

history_physical.valid_request_if: Asked for history, HPI, review of systems, or physical examination.
history_physical.content: The patient reports a painless, slowly enlarging lesion on the left dorsal forearm present for about 8 months. No bleeding, pruritus, or rapid change. On exam there is an 8 mm pearly papule with a rolled border and central crust. Arborizing telangiectasias are visible on close inspection. The lesion is non-tender. No regional lymphadenopathy. Extensive lifetime sun exposure. No prior skin cancers.

dermoscopy.valid_request_if: Asked for dermoscopy or dermatoscopy findings.
dermoscopy.content: Dermoscopy shows arborizing telangiectasias, shiny white structures, and focal ulceration. No pigment network is seen.

biopsy_pathology.valid_request_if: Asked for biopsy, histopathology, or pathology report.
biopsy_pathology.content: Shave biopsy of the left forearm lesion reveals nodular basal cell carcinoma. Tumor islands with peripheral palisading and stromal retraction are present. No perineural invasion is identified. Diagnosis basal cell carcinoma, nodular type.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Suspected nonmelanoma skin cancer, likely basal cell carcinoma

treatment_plan.action: Avoid destructive treatment before tissue diagnosis

key_findings_quotes: a painless, slowly enlarging lesion on the left dorsal forearm
an 8 mm pearly papule with a rolled border and central crust
Arborizing telangiectasias are visible on close inspection

treatment_plan.action: Perform shave biopsy of the lesion for histologic confirmation
Prepare for simple surgical excision under local anesthesia if basal cell carcinoma is confirmed

key_findings_quotes: Dermoscopy shows arborizing telangiectasias, shiny white structures, and focal ulceration

round_3.working_diagnosis: Nodular basal cell carcinoma of the left forearm, low-risk

treatment_plan.action: Simple surgical excision with 4 mm clinical margins and pathology margin assessment
Schedule full skin examination follow-up at 6 to 12 months

key_findings_quotes: Shave biopsy of the left forearm lesion reveals nodular basal cell carcinoma
Tumor islands with peripheral palisading and stromal retraction are present
